correct.description: The Manufacturing folder contains only drill maps (NPTH/PTH drl_map.gbr) and drill files — no copper, mask, silk, or Edge.Cuts gerbers. The analyzer correctly reports complete=false, found_layers=[], missing_required=[B.Cu, B.Mask, Edge.Cuts, F.Cu, F.Mask], and board_dimensions={} (no Edge.Cuts available). Real design issue: designer exported only drill files.

findings.summary: BT136-500 TRIAC (Q1) classified as 'transistor' instead of 'triac'; PS1/PS2 multi-driver GND false positive — two independent PSUs sharing GND is normal; LDO thermal dissipation warning correctly f...

correct.description: U1 LM1117-3.3 receives 12V input and outputs 3.3V. With ESP32-WROOM-32 (240mA) + PCA9685BS (10mA) = 250mA, the dropout is 8.7V giving 2175mW dissipation. LM1117 in SOT-223 is typically rated 800-1500mW. The analyzer correctly identifies this as a thermal concern. Real design risk.

incorrect.description: Q1 BT136-500 is a bidirectional TRIAC (used for AC switching via the MOC3021M optocoupler in the optocoupler AC section). The component_types reports 'transistor: 4' grouping Q1 BT136-500 with Q2/Q3/Q4 (2N7000 MOSFETs). TRIACs have fundamentally different behavior and should be classified separately. The footprint is TO-220-3.

connectivity_issues reports a multi_driver_nets warning for GND with PS1 (IRM-60-12, -Vout) and PS2 (IRM-60-24, -Vout) both driving GND as power_out. Two independent AC/DC supplies sharing a common ground is standard practice and should not be flagged. The IRM-60 series share a common ground reference in this design, which is intentional.

findings.summary: U4 QFN thermal pad with zero thermal vias correctly detected; U5 ESP32 thermal pad via count flagged as insufficient due to small via drill (0.20mm); DFM board_size violation correctly flagged: 134...

correct.description: U4 PCA9685BS is a QFN-28 with a 4.25x4.25mm exposed pad (pad 29). The analyzer correctly reports 0 thermal vias (recommended min 9, ideal 16) with adequacy='none'. This is a real DFM issue — without thermal vias the pad cannot dissipate heat to the back copper.

U5 has 12 via pads but effective count is 5.3 (below the 9 minimum threshold) because the via drill is 0.20mm. The analyzer correctly explains the small drill size penalty and notes the design may follow the manufacturer's recommended via pattern. This is a nuanced and accurate finding.

The PCB is 134.366x106.68mm which exceeds the 100x100mm JLCPCB standard tier boundary. The analyzer correctly reports this as a pricing tier violation requiring the 'standard' tier. Accurate DFM finding.
